subject: Fix stale internal CELL-N print headers to match current Step numbering
description: In 02_thesis_data/_02_preprocessing/nielsen/CSD/pipeline_step_scripts/pre_processing_notebook_csd.ipynb, several code cells' internal print("CELL N: ...") header strings are stale/mismatched against the markdown Step-numbering fixed in this session (e.g. the cell now under markdown header 'Step 3.14 - ACF/PACF Analysis' prints 'CELL 11: ACF/PACF Analysis...', colliding with Step 3.12's own 'CELL 11: Cross-Brand Heterogeneity Analysis' print). This is cosmetic only (doesn't affect execution or data), but confusing when reading notebook output top-to-bottom. Walk every code cell under the 'Step 3: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)' section, find its print("CELL N...") or print("CELL N ...") header line, and renumber/rename it to match that cell's own markdown header exactly (e.g. Step 3.14's cell should print something like 'STEP 3.14: ACF/PACF Analysis (Autocorrelation Structure)', matching the pattern used by Step 4.1-4.5's print("STEP 4.X: ...") headers, not the old ad-hoc 'CELL N' scheme carried over from before the EDA/pipeline merge). Verify by grepping the notebook JSON for 'print("CELL' after the fix (should return zero matches in the EDA section) and re-running the full notebook to confirm no print statements broke.
activeForm: Fixing stale internal CELL-N print headers
